scope_boundary: Only the bounded Project Theseus simulation-fidelity receipt-suite import moves to prototype-backed: a sanitized public-safe fixture records a GREEN local Theseus report with 5/5 passed simulation-fidelity fixtures, 6 simulation contract records, 6 fidelity records, 6 world-adapter receipts, 6 evidence-transition records, 6 failure-boundary records, 1 blocked transfer, 1 downgraded claim, 1 scenario-only record, zero public training rows, zero external inference calls, and seven expected-invalid controls. This does not promote the Resource Economics chapter core claim, simulator adequacy, physical feasibility, benchmark transfer, native KV parity, deployment readiness, live simulator behavior, model quality, economic outcome, learned generation, clean live Project Theseus replay, safety, alignment, transfer, or ASI.
transition_reason: The imported Theseus report records a concrete simulation-fidelity receipt suite rather than only a proposed simulation-fidelity vocabulary: it distinguishes narrow invariant support, benchmark-adapter limits, scenario-only support, blocked native-KV transfer, downgraded route-shape evidence, and a bounded compile-time planning world adapter. The support movement is therefore limited to this non-core import claim and remains prototype-backed rather than empirical-test-backed or chapter-core evidence.
